statement.markdown: You are given a function that generates Fibonacci numbers up to the nth number. However, it has a logical bug that causes it to return incorrect results for non-positive inputs. Fix the bug to make it work correctly for all positive integers.
statement.inputFormat: The function takes an integer n as input, representing the number of Fibonacci numbers to generate.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return an array of Fibonacci numbers up to the nth number.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input n will always be a positive integer.

constraints.description: The number of Fibonacci numbers to generate

examples.explanation: The first 5 Fibonacci numbers are 0, 1, 1, 2, and 3
The first 8 Fibonacci numbers are 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, and 13

hints.text: Think about how to handle non-positive inputs correctly

editorial.title: Correcting the Fibonacci Sequence Generation
editorial.intuition: To fix the bug, you need to handle non-positive inputs correctly. One way to do this is to add a simple check at the beginning of the function to return an empty array for non-positive inputs.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to use a simple loop to generate Fibonacci numbers one by one.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use a dynamic programming approach to generate Fibonacci numbers in a more efficient way.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a dynamic programming approach with memoization to generate Fibonacci numbers in an efficient way.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness proof is based on the fact that the dynamic programming approach with memoization correctly implements the Fibonacci sequence generation.
